item.title: Debugging with gdb
item.body:

(exec'd /home/gh-zjp-CN/.rustup/toolchains/nightly-2025-12-06-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/bin/cargo),

There was also a closed history disccusion in miri repo that was not helpful
* https://github.com/rust-lang/miri/issues/496

Downloading separate debug info for /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libgcc_s.so.1 [Thread debugging using libthread_db enabled] Using host libthread_db library "/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libthread_db.so.1". Thread 30.1 "miri" hit Breakpoint 2.5, miri::main () at src/bin/miri.rs:440 440 let early_dcx = EarlyDiagCtxt::new(ErrorOutputType::default()); ```

It needs hitting more exec catch points to jump in `miri::eval::create_ecx`. Seems the processes of cargo-miri and miri communicate several times before real MIR interpretation.
The 103th inferior is the miri process that hits the break point `miri::eval::create_ecx` and starts interpretation.

KMiri has some scripts to launch GDB to debug the process of miri analysis.

See the `tool/gdb/` folder and entry.gdb in tests.

```bash
# bash command style
KMiri/tests/init $ OSDK_LOCAL_DEV=1 rust-gdb -x ../../tool/gdb/miri.gdb --args cargo osdk miri test
```

or plain GDB command style:

```gdb
# entry.gdb
set environment OSDK_LOCAL_DEV=1
file cargo
set args osdk miri test
source ../../tool/gdb/miri.gdb
```
